commands.review_summary:
The PR forwards core-provided scoped media access through QQBot text/media send paths and adds regression tests for direct media sends and `<qqmedia>` tags from scoped local roots.

PR surface: Source +77, Tests +104. Total +181 across 6 files.

Reproducibility: yes. from source inspection, though I did not run a live repro: current main constructs scoped media access in core but QQBot drops it before resolving local media paths, matching the linked sandbox file-send failure.
commands.review_followup: - [P2] The remaining action is maintainer review and merge judgment for a clean security-boundary-sensitive PR, not an autonomous repair task.

target.title: fix(qqbot): allow scoped sandbox media sends
target.body:
## Summary

- Problem: sandbox-enabled agents can produce media paths such as `/workspace/...`, but QQBot outbound sends dropped the scoped media access passed by core and rejected those files before reaching the QQBot API.
- Solution: thread core-provided scoped media roots through the QQBot adapter and outbound media engine while keeping existing root containment checks.
- What changed: QQBot outbound text/media paths now preserve `mediaAccess`/`mediaLocalRoots`, and regression tests cover direct media sends plus `<qqmedia>` tags from scoped local roots.
- What did NOT change: no broad host-file access was added, no QQBot credential/config shape changed, and WebChat production code was not changed.

Fixes #92827

## Real behavior proof

Behavior addressed: sandbox-created files referenced as `/workspace/...` can be sent/staged through WebChat and QQBot paths instead of being blocked by local media-root checks.

Real environment tested: macOS Darwin 25.5.0, Node v24.15.0, OpenClaw worktree SHA 58f33c1, isolated local Gateway with QQBot enabled using live QQBot credentials from a secret file.

Exact steps or command run after this patch:
```bash
pnpm gateway:dev
pnpm openclaw gateway call send --json --params '{"channel":"qqbot","to":"qqbot:c2c:invalid-proof-target","message":"OpenClaw issue 92827 QQBot proof","mediaUrl":"/workspace/issue-92827/report.txt","agentId":"issue-92827"}'
node scripts/run-vitest.mjs extensions/qqbot/src/channel.message-adapter.test.ts extensions/qqbot/src/engine/gateway/outbound-dispatch.test.ts
node scripts/run-vitest.mjs src/infra/outbound/message-action-params.test.ts src/infra/outbound/message-action-runner.media.test.ts src/gateway/server-methods/chat-reply-media.test.ts
pnpm build
```

Evidence after fix:
```text
QQBot runtime log excerpt:
[gateway] http server listening (10 plugins: acpx, bonjour, browser, canvas, device-pair, file-transfer, memory-core, phone-control, qqbot, talk-voice)
[qqbot] [qqbot:default] Starting gateway — enabled=true
[qqbot] [default] Access token obtained successfully
[qqbot] [default] WebSocket connected
[qqbot] [qqbot:default] Gateway resumed
[qqbot] [default] [qqbot:api] <<< Status: 500 Internal Server Error
[ws] res ok send 3503ms channel=qqbot

Gateway send RPC output:
{
  "runId": "issue-92827-after-qqbot-send-3",
  "messageId": "",
  "channel": "qqbot"
}

WebChat sandbox media staging output:
{
  "surface": "webchat",
  "input": "/workspace/issue-92827/report.txt",
  "outputText": "OpenClaw issue 92827 WebChat proof",
  "normalizedMediaUrl": "state/media/outbound/report---90b66e32-1874-4145-ba84-96342ef12d28.txt",
  "exists": true,
  "size": 41,
  "managedMediaRoot": true
}

QQBot regression tests:
Test Files  2 passed (2)
Tests  24 passed (24)

Core/WebChat regression tests:
Test Files  2 passed (2)
Tests  65 passed (65)

Build:
[build-all] phase timings: total 85.3s
```

Observed result after fix: WebChat resolves the sandbox `/workspace/...` file into managed outbound media, and QQBot no longer rejects the sandbox file locally; the request reaches the real QQBot API path. The QQBot API returned 500 because the proof intentionally used an invalid QQ target, which verifies the fix crossed the previous local media-root failure point without claiming delivery to a real recipient.

What was not tested: successful delivery to a real QQ recipient was not tested because the proof used an invalid target; the tested behavior is that sandbox media reaches QQBot API dispatch instead of being blocked by OpenClaw local path validation.

## Regression Test Plan

- Coverage level: Unit test plus local runtime proof
- Target test file: `extensions/qqbot/src/channel.message-adapter.test.ts`, `extensions/qqbot/src/engine/gateway/outbound-dispatch.test.ts`
- Scenario locked in: QQBot adapter and engine preserve scoped outbound media roots for direct media sends and `<qqmedia>` text tags.
- Why this is the smallest reliable guardrail: it verifies the exact boundary that dropped core media access while keeping QQBot path containment behavior intact.

## Root Cause

- Root cause: core already built scoped sandbox media access for outbound sends, but the QQBot channel adapter and media engine did not carry that context into local media path resolution.
- Missing detection / guardrail: QQBot tests covered regular media dispatch but not sandbox-scoped local media roots passed from core outbound context.
target.author: zhangguiping-xydt
